questions.question:
The determinant of the matrix \( \begin{bmatrix} 6 & -8 & 1 & 1 \\ 0 & 2 & 4 & 6 \\ 0 & 0 & 4 & 8 \\ 0 & 0 & 0 & -1 \end{bmatrix} \)



GATE CSE 1997 | Question: 1.3

The determinant of the matrix \[ \begin{bmatrix}2 &0  &0  &0 \\  8&  1&  7& 2\\  2&  0&2  &0 \\  9&0  & 6 & 1 \end{bmatrix} \]



GATE CSE 2000 | Question: 1.3

Which one of the following does NOT equal \[ \begin{vmatrix} 1 & x & x^{2}\\ 1& y & y^{2}\\ 1 & z & z^{2} \end{vmatrix} \quad ? \]



GATE CSE 2013 | Question: 3
options: \( \begin{vmatrix} 1& x(x+1)& x+1\\ 1& y(y+1) & y+1\\ 1& z(z+1) & z+1 \end{vmatrix} \)
\( \begin{vmatrix} 1& x+1 & x^{2}+1\\ 1& y+1 & y^{2}+1\\ 1& z+1 & z^{2}+1 \end{vmatrix} \)
\( \begin{vmatrix} 0& x-y & x^{2}-y^{2}\\ 0 & y-z & y^{2}-z^{2}\\ 1 & z & z^{2} \end{vmatrix} \)
\( \begin{vmatrix} 2& x+y & x^{2}+y^{2}\\ 2 & y+z & y^{2}+z^{2}\\ 1 & z & z^{2} \end{vmatrix} \)
questions.correct_answer: \( \begin{vmatrix} 1& x(x+1)& x+1\\ 1& y(y+1) & y+1\\ 1& z(z+1) & z+1 \end{vmatrix} \)

questions.question:
If the matrix \( A \) is such that \[ A= \begin{bmatrix} 2\\ −4\\7\end{bmatrix}\begin{bmatrix}1& 9& 5\end{bmatrix} \] then the determinant of \( A \) is equal to ______.


GATE CSE 2014 Set 2 | Question: 4

Let \( X \) be a square matrix. Consider the following two statements on \( X \).

  1. \( X \) is invertible
  2. Determinant of \( X \) is non-zero

Which one of the following is TRUE?



GATE CSE 2019 | Question: 9
options: I implies II; II does not imply I
II implies I; I does not imply II
I does not imply II; II does not imply I

questions.question:
Let
\[
A=\left[\begin{array}{llll}
1 & 2 & 3 & 4 \\
4 & 1 & 2 & 3 \\
3 & 4 & 1 & 2 \\
2 & 3 & 4 & 1
\end{array}\right]
\]
and
\[
B=\left[\begin{array}{llll}
3 & 4 & 1 & 2 \\
4 & 1 & 2 & 3 \\
1 & 2 & 3 & 4 \\
2 & 3 & 4 & 1
\end{array}\right]
\]
Let \( \operatorname{det}(A) \) and \( \operatorname{det}(B) \) denote the determinants of the matrices \( A \) and \( B, \) respectively.

Which one of the options given below is \( \text{TRUE}? \)



GATE CSE 2023 | Question: 8

​​​​​Let \( A \) be an \( n \times n \) matrix over the set of all real numbers \( \mathbb{R} \). Let \( B \) be a matrix obtained from \( A \) by swapping two rows. Which of the following statements is/are TRUE?



GATE CSE 2024 | Set 2 | Question: 37
options: The determinant of \( B \) is the negative of the determinant of \( A \)
If \( A \) is invertible, then \( B \) is also invertible
If \( A \) is symmetric, then \( B \) is also symmetric
If the trace of \( A \) is zero, then the trace of \( B \) is also zero
correct_answer: The determinant of \( B \) is the negative of the determinant of \( A \)
If \( A \) is invertible, then \( B \) is also invertible

questions.question:
Let \( L \), \( M \), and \( N \) be non-singular matrices of order \( 3 \) satisfying the equations \( L^2 = L^{-1} \), \( M=L^8 \) and \( N=L^2 \). 

Which ONE of the following is the value of the deteminant of \( (M-N) \)?


GATE CSE 2025 | Set 2 | Question: 4

Consider the \( 3 \times 3 \) matrix \( \boldsymbol{M}=\left[\begin{array}{lll}1 & 2 & 3 \\ 3 & 1 & 3 \\ 4 & 3 & 6\end{array}\right] \).

The determinant of \( \left(\boldsymbol{M}^{2}+12 \boldsymbol{M}\right) \) is \( \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_ \).


GATE DS&AI 2024 | Question: 25

Let \( A \) be an \( n \times n \) matrix of the following form.

\[ A = \begin{bmatrix}3&1&0&0&0&\ldots&0&0&0\\
1&3&1&0&0&\ldots&0&0&0\\
0&1&3&1&0&\ldots&0&0&0\\
0&0&1&3&1&\ldots&0&0&0\\
\ldots\\
\ldots \\
0&0&0&0&0&\ldots&1&3&1\\
0&0&0&0&0&\ldots&0&1&3\\
  \end{bmatrix}_{n\times n} \]
What is the value of the determinant of \( A \)?



GATE IT 2004 | Question: 32

The determinant of the matrix given below is

\[ \begin{bmatrix}
0 &1  &0  &2 \\
 -1&  1&  1& 3\\
 0&0  &0  & 1\\
 1&  -2&  0& 1
\end{bmatrix} \]



GATE IT 2005 | Question: 3
